ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: Order created successfully via UI when order date, customer, and one line item are provided
classification_rationale: Validates the happy-path business rule that an order is created when all required fields are populated via the UI.
preconditions: Order Management application is deployed and accessible in UAT environment.
User account 'sales_user_01' exists with role Sales_Representative and has create permission on Order module.
Customer record CUST-10045 (Acme Corp) exists in the system in Active status.
Product SKU-00112 (Wireless Mouse) exists with available inventory of 500 units.
test_data: Line Item 1 — Product: SKU-00112 (Wireless Mouse), Quantity: 3, Unit Price: $24.99
steps: Navigate to the Create Order page from the Order Management menu.
Enter '2025-07-15' in the Order Date field.
Search for and select customer 'CUST-10045 (Acme Corp)' in the Customer field.
Click the Add Line Item button.
Enter product code 'SKU-00112' in the Product field of line item row 1.
Enter '3' in the Quantity field of line item row 1.
Click the Submit Order button.
expected_result: Order is created successfully. A confirmation message 'Order created successfully' is displayed. A new Order ID is generated and shown on screen. The order summary displays Order Date as 2025-07-15, Customer as Acme Corp (CUST-10045), and one line item for SKU-00112 with quantity 3 and line total of $74.97.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: API creates order successfully when all required fields and one line item are provided in POST request
classification_rationale: Validates the happy-path business rule at the API layer to ensure the create order endpoint accepts a well-formed request with all required fields.
preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible at https://api.uat.example.com/v1/orders.
Valid OAuth2 bearer token exists for user 'sales_user_01' with role Sales_Representative and create permission on Order resource.
Customer record CUST-10045 (Acme Corp) exists in Active status.
Product SKU-00250 (USB-C Hub) exists with unit price $39.99 and available inventory of 200 units.
steps: Send a POST request to /v1/orders with the request body containing order_date, customer_id, and one line item.
Capture the HTTP response status code and body.
expected_result: HTTP 201 Created is returned. Response body contains a generated 'order_id' (non-null, format ORD-XXXXX), 'order_date' as '2025-07-15', 'customer_id' as 'CUST-10045', 'status' as 'Created', and a 'line_items' array with one entry containing product_code 'SKU-00250', quantity 5, and unit_price 39.99.
review_feedback: completeness: Missing execution detail or contains quality violations

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

completeness.reason: Missing execution detail or contains quality violations

expected_result: HTTP 201 Created is returned. Response body contains a generated 'order_id', 'order_date' as '2025-08-01', 'customer_id' as 'CUST-20078', and a 'line_items' array with exactly 2 entries matching the submitted products and quantities.

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.

steps: Leave the Order Date field blank.
Leave the Customer field blank.
Do not add any line items.
Click the Submit Order button.
expected_result: The order is not created. Inline validation errors are displayed: 'Order Date is required' next to the Order Date field, 'Customer is required' next to the Customer field, and 'At least one line item is required' in the line items section. The Submit Order button remains on screen and no Order ID is generated.

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: Unauthenticated request to create order endpoint is rejected with 401
classification_rationale: Non-functional security test validating that the create order endpoint enforces authentication before processing required-field validation.
preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible at https://api.uat.example.com/v1/orders.
No valid authentication token is provided in the request.
steps: Send a POST request to /v1/orders without an Authorization header.
Capture the HTTP response status code and body.
expected_result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned. Response body contains error message 'Authentication required'. No order_id is generated and no order record is created in the system.

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: No order record is persisted in database when required fields are missing and API returns 400
classification_rationale: Validates data integrity at the database layer to ensure a rejected order request does not leave partial or orphan records.
preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible.
Direct read access to the Order Management database (schema: order_mgmt, tables: orders, order_line_items) is available via SQL client.
Current maximum order_id in orders table is ORD-99100.
Product SKU-00112 exists in product catalog.
test_data: Request Body (missing customer_id and empty line_items): { "order_date": "2025-07-15", "line_items": [] }
SQL Query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_mgmt.orders WHERE order_id > 'ORD-99100'
steps: Record the current row count in the orders table where order_id > 'ORD-99100'.
Send a POST request to /v1/orders with missing customer_id and an empty line_items array.
Execute SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_mgmt.orders WHERE order_id > 'ORD-99100'.
Execute SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_mgmt.order_line_items WHERE order_id > 'ORD-99100'.
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 400. The row count in orders table where order_id > 'ORD-99100' remains 0 (unchanged from before the request). The row count in order_line_items for corresponding order_ids also remains 0. No partial data is persisted.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

ac_text: Invalid product codes must be rejected.

classification_rationale: Validates that the database-level foreign key constraint on product_code prevents persistence of invalid product codes.
preconditions: Database is accessible in UAT environment with read/write credentials for QA schema user qa_db_user.

A valid order header record ORD-TEST-9001 exists in the Orders table for customer CUST-20045.
steps: Connect to the UAT database as qa_db_user.
Execute INSERT INTO Order_Line_Items (order_id, product_code, quantity, unit_price) VALUES ('ORD-TEST-9001', 'BOGUS-00001', 4, 10.00).
expected_result: The INSERT statement fails with a foreign key constraint violation error referencing the Products table. No row is inserted into the Order_Line_Items table for product code BOGUS-00001. A SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Order_Line_Items WHERE order_id = 'ORD-TEST-9001' AND product_code = 'BOGUS-00001' returns 0.

Wait for the job to complete.
Query the target Orders and Order_Line_Items tables for order_id BATCH-ORD-3001.
Query the etl_error_log table for records with source_order_id BATCH-ORD-3001.
expected_result: No record with order_id BATCH-ORD-3001 is present in the target Orders or Order_Line_Items tables. The etl_error_log table contains one entry with source_order_id BATCH-ORD-3001, error_type 'INVALID_PRODUCT_CODE', and error_message referencing product code 'DEL-00000'. Review the order summary section.
Click the Submit Order button.

Order creation transaction has committed successfully.
test_data: Expected row in order_line_items table: order_id='ORD-88901', product_code='SKU-2050', quantity=1, unit_price=329.00, tax_rate=0.0825, tax_amount=27.14, line_total=356.14

steps: Execute query: SELECT product_code, quantity, unit_price, tax_rate, tax_amount, line_total FROM order_line_items WHERE order_id = 'ORD-88901'.
Execute query: SELECT subtotal, total_tax, total FROM orders WHERE order_id = 'ORD-88901'.
expected_result: The order_line_items row returns product_code 'SKU-2050', quantity 1, unit_price 329.00, tax_rate 0.0825, tax_amount 27.14, and line_total 356.14. title: Order created via UI triggers event visible in downstream fulfillment integration dashboard
classification_rationale: Validates the happy-path UI order creation flow produces an observable integration event, verifying a single feature's behavior.
preconditions: Order Management web application is accessible at https://uat.example.com/orders

ac_text: Order creation event must be available to downstream integrations.

preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible at https://api.uat.example.com/v1/orders

User account exists with role Sales_Agent and has create permission on Order module, API key: APIKEY-SA-77012

Product SKU-A1001 (Wireless Mouse, unit price $25.99, taxable) exists in the product catalog with available inventory of 500 units
steps: Send POST request to /v1/orders with the order payload containing customer_id CUST-10045, order_date 2025-07-15, and one line item for SKU-A1001 quantity 2.
Capture the returned order_id from the HTTP 201 response.
Connect to RabbitMQ management API and consume the next message from queue 'order.created'.
Parse the consumed message body as JSON.
expected_result: HTTP 201 is returned with a generated order_id. A message is present on queue 'order.created' within 5 seconds. The message body contains: event_type='ORDER_CREATED', order_id matching the returned value, customer_id='CUST-10045', order_date='2025-07-15', line_items array with one entry for product_code 'SKU-A1001', quantity 2, and unit_price 25.99. The message includes a timestamp in ISO-8601 format and a correlation_id.

ac_text: Order creation event must be available to downstream integrations.

classification_rationale: Validates the failure recovery behavior of the integration event publishing mechanism, which is a single-feature exception handling rule.
preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible at https://api.uat.example.com/v1/orders
Message broker (RabbitMQ) is intentionally stopped or the 'order.created' queue is unreachable (simulated by network policy block on port 5672)

Customer CUST-10045 (Acme Corp) and product SKU-A1001 (Wireless Mouse, $25.99) exist
test_data: Expected event status: FAILED or RETRY_PENDING
steps: Confirm the message broker is unreachable by verifying connectivity test to port 5672 fails.
Send POST request to /v1/orders with order_date 2025-07-15, customer_id CUST-10045, and line item SKU-A1001 quantity 1.
Capture the returned order_id from the response.
Wait 90 seconds for retry attempts to exhaust.
Query ORDER_DB.order_events WHERE order_id = '<captured_order_id>' to check event status and retry_count.
Query ORDER_DB.order_event_errors WHERE order_id = '<captured_order_id>' for error log entries.
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 201 with a valid order_id (order is created regardless of event delivery). The order_events record shows status 'FAILED' or 'RETRY_PENDING' with retry_count equal to 3. The order_event_errors table contains at least one error entry with error_message referencing connection failure to the message broker and a timestamp within the last 90 seconds. No message exists on the 'order.created' queue.

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: Rapid consecutive API requests to transition cancelled order to Shipped are all rejected — no race condition bypass
classification_rationale: Validates the business rule holds under concurrent request conditions at the API layer, ensuring no race condition allows the invalid transition.
preconditions: API endpoint PUT /api/v1/orders/{orderId}/status is deployed and accessible.
Valid API bearer tokens exist for service accounts svc_ops_api_01 and svc_ops_api_02, both with role Operations_Manager.
Order ORD-50321 exists in the database with status Cancelled.
steps: Send 5 simultaneous PUT requests to /api/v1/orders/ORD-50321/status with body {"status": "Shipped"} using parallel threads.
Collect all 5 response codes and response bodies.
Query the database: SELECT status FROM orders WHERE order_id = 'ORD-50321'.
expected_result: All 5 responses return HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity with error INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION. The database query returns status Cancelled for ORD-50321. No response returns HTTP 200. The order_status_history table contains zero records with new_status = 'Shipped' for ORD-50321.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Order status change from Pending to Shipped reflects in reporting dashboard and triggers email notification
classification_rationale: Validates the core business rule that a status update is visible in both reporting and notifications via the UI layer.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250101 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-10045 (Acme Corp)
User account 'ops_user_01' exists with role Operations_Manager and has update permission on Order Status module
Notification service is configured to send email to ops_notify@acmecorp.com on order status change
Reporting module is deployed and accessible at /reports/order-status
steps: Navigate to Order Management > Order Details for ORD-20250101
Select 'Shipped' from the Status dropdown
Click the 'Update Status' button
Navigate to Reports > Order Status Summary
Enter Order ID ORD-20250101 in the report filter field
Click 'Generate Report'
Open the notification inbox for ops_notify@acmecorp.com
expected_result: Order ORD-20250101 displays status 'Shipped' with timestamp in the Order Status Summary report. An email notification is delivered to ops_notify@acmecorp.com with subject 'Order ORD-20250101 Status Updated to Shipped' and body containing order ID, new status, and update timestamp.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: API status update to In Transit persists in reporting data and publishes notification event
classification_rationale: Validates the same AC via API layer to ensure programmatic status updates also propagate to reporting and notifications.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250202 exists with current status 'Processing' for customer CUST-10078 (Beta Industries)
API authentication token is valid for service account 'svc_ops_api' with scope order:update
Notification message broker topic 'order-status-updates' is active and subscribable
Reporting API endpoint GET /api/v1/reports/orders/{orderId}/status is deployed
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250202/status with the request body and authorization header
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/orders/ORD-20250202/status
Subscribe to message broker topic 'order-status-updates' and retrieve the latest message for ORD-20250202
expected_result: PUT returns HTTP 200 with response body containing {"order_id": "ORD-20250202", "status": "InTransit"}. GET reporting endpoint returns status 'InTransit' with updated_at '2025-07-15T10:30:00Z'. Notification topic contains a message with order_id 'ORD-20250202', new_status 'InTransit', and event_type 'ORDER_STATUS_CHANGED'.

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Alternative valid status update to Delivered generates reporting entry and SMS notification
classification_rationale: Validates an alternate valid status transition to ensure all terminal statuses also propagate to reporting and notifications.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250303 exists with current status 'Shipped' for customer CUST-10092 (Gamma LLC)
Customer CUST-10092 has SMS notification preference enabled with phone +1-555-0187
API authentication token is valid for service account 'svc_ops_api' with scope order:update
Reporting data warehouse is online and accepting new status records
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250303/status with the request body and authorization header
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/orders/ORD-20250303/status
Query the notification log API GET /api/v1/notifications?order_id=ORD-20250303&type=SMS
expected_result: PUT returns HTTP 200 with status 'Delivered'. Reporting endpoint returns order ORD-20250303 with status 'Delivered' and updated_at '2025-07-15T14:00:00Z'. Notification log shows an SMS sent to +1-555-0187 with message containing 'Order ORD-20250303 has been Delivered'.

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: ETL pipeline extracts status update events and loads them into reporting data warehouse within SLA
classification_rationale: Validates that the ETL pipeline correctly extracts, transforms, and loads status change events into the reporting layer for downstream visibility.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250606 status was updated from 'Pending' to 'Shipped' at 2025-07-15T09:00:00Z in the transactional database
ETL job 'order_status_sync' is scheduled to run every 15 minutes
Reporting data warehouse table 'rpt_order_status' is empty for ORD-20250606
ETL pipeline has source connection to transactional DB and target connection to reporting warehouse
steps: Trigger the ETL job 'order_status_sync' manually
Wait for the job to complete
Query reporting.rpt_order_status WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250606'
expected_result: ETL job completes with status SUCCESS. Query returns 1 row with order_id 'ORD-20250606', status 'Shipped', source_updated_at '2025-07-15T09:00:00Z', and etl_loaded_at within 15 minutes of the source timestamp. No data truncation or transformation errors are logged.
review_feedback: data_integrity: Data integrity checks are weak

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: UI reporting shows no status update row when invalid order ID is filtered
classification_rationale: Validates that the reporting UI handles non-existent order gracefully and does not display phantom status data.
preconditions: No order exists with ID ORD-99999999 in the system
User account 'ops_user_01' exists with role Operations_Manager and has read permission on Reporting module
Reporting module is deployed and accessible at /reports/order-status
steps: Navigate to Reports > Order Status Summary
Enter Order ID ORD-99999999 in the report filter field
Click 'Generate Report'
expected_result: Report displays an empty result set with message 'No records found for Order ID ORD-99999999'. No status rows, timestamps, or notification entries are shown.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Status update with disabled notification service still persists in reporting but logs notification failure
classification_rationale: Validates rejection/failure handling when notification subsystem is unavailable, ensuring reporting still reflects the update.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250404 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-10055 (Delta Corp)
Notification service endpoint is intentionally offline or returns HTTP 503
API authentication token is valid for service account 'svc_ops_api' with scope order:update
Reporting data warehouse is online
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250404/status with the request body and authorization header
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/orders/ORD-20250404/status
Query the application error log for notification failures related to ORD-20250404
expected_result: PUT returns HTTP 200 with a warning field: {"notification_status": "FAILED", "reason": "Notification service unavailable"}. Reporting endpoint returns order ORD-20250404 with status 'Processing' and correct timestamp. Application error log contains entry with level ERROR, order_id 'ORD-20250404', and message 'Failed to send notification: service unavailable'.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Unauthorised API request to update order status is rejected and no reporting or notification records are created
classification_rationale: Non-functional security test ensuring that unauthorized access does not result in phantom reporting or notification data.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250707 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-10070 (Foxtrot Inc)
API request uses an expired authentication token: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.expired_token_2024
Reporting and notification audit tables are baseline-checked for ORD-20250707
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250707/status with the expired authorization header and request body
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/orders/ORD-20250707/status
Send GET request to /api/v1/notifications?order_id=ORD-20250707
expected_result: PUT returns HTTP 401 with response body {"error": "Unauthorized", "message": "Authentication token is expired or invalid"}. Reporting endpoint still shows status 'Pending' for ORD-20250707 with no new timestamp entries. Notification query returns no new notification records for ORD-20250707.

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Database records status history and notification audit trail after rapid consecutive status updates
classification_rationale: Validates that rapid consecutive status changes are each individually recorded in the reporting tables and notification audit log at the database level.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250505 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-10060 (Echo Enterprises)
Database tables 'order_status_history' and 'notification_audit_log' are accessible

Three consecutive status updates have been submitted via API within 5 seconds: Pending→Processing at 2025-07-15T12:00:00Z, Processing→Shipped at 2025-07-15T12:00:02Z, Shipped→Delivered at 2025-07-15T12:00:04Z
test_data: Query: SELECT status, updated_at FROM order_status_history WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250505' ORDER BY updated_at ASC
Query: SELECT event_type, status, sent_at FROM notification_audit_log WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250505' ORDER BY sent_at ASC
steps: Execute the order_status_history query against the reporting database
Execute the notification_audit_log query against the reporting database
expected_result: order_status_history returns exactly 3 rows: (Processing, 2025-07-15T12:00:00Z), (Shipped, 2025-07-15T12:00:02Z), (Delivered, 2025-07-15T12:00:04Z). notification_audit_log returns exactly 3 rows each with event_type 'ORDER_STATUS_CHANGED' and matching status and timestamp values. No rows are missing or duplicated.
